observed_response: Copilot chat bubble rendered Korean warning with full sentence: '⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다. (This request has been blocked due to sensitive information.)' Contains exact '차단' substring. NO 'Something went wrong' generic English fallback. Bubble appears under standard 'Copilot' bot header (Copilot avatar + name + body text in normal assistant bubble layout). Conversation URL advanced to /chat/conversation/dc46ca03-c461-4da8-95a5-f9424e01f7b3 (real persisted conversation, not error state). Composer 'Copilot에 메시지 보내기' re-enabled (focused, value empty). Single [피드백 보내기] action button rendered below the warning (no [다시 시도] retry button — distinct from the FAIL pattern of #1138/#1146/#1164/#1174/#1175 which always showed both retry+feedback). Page title transitioned from '채팅 | M365 Copilot' to 'M365 Copilot' indicating conversation save.
envelope_strategy: 3-frame burst — (A) type:1 update with messages[].text + isLastUpdate:true, (B) type:2 Completion echoing invocationId from c2s type:4 ('0' fallback), (C) type:3 Completion-ACK; invocation_id parsed from ws_c2s_accumulated_buffer; conversation_id parsed from URL ?ConversationId=

comparison_to_prior_fails.1138_1146_1164: All prior wave 0 attempts emitted generic 'Something went wrong. Please try again later.' English fallback with [다시 시도][피드백 보내기] retry pair. NO Korean text anywhere in DOM.
comparison_to_prior_fails.1174: Task #8 H2 correlation_id capture-at-register_domain_block + WS-inject-fallback. Same 'Something went wrong' English fallback. Engine fired but client rejected/swallowed payload — frame format mismatch suspected.
comparison_to_prior_fails.1175: Task #10 M365SignalrHandler migration (handler_class activated via DB). Same 'Something went wrong' English fallback — handler migration was no-op refactor, delegated to legacy render_envelope.
comparison_to_prior_fails.1176: Engine OFF baseline HAR capture — captured 14 WS frames from substrate.office.com hub: handshake → c2s type:4 StreamInvocation invocationId:'0' → 7 s2c type:1 update chunks (last one with isLastUpdate:true) → s2c type:2 COMPLETION → s2c type:3 ACK. Identified the 3-frame closure sequence (type:1+isLastUpdate, type:2 Completion, type:3 ACK) that the iter5 envelope rewrite mimics.
comparison_to_prior_fails.1177_pass_root_cause: Envelope rewrite to 3-frame burst now matches the M365 SignalR client's expected stream termination contract. Client accepts isLastUpdate:true on type:1 as 'stream complete, render final text', receives type:2 with matching invocationId as Completion confirmation, type:3 as ACK closure. No more 'incomplete stream' or 'protocol violation' detection by client → no fallback to 'Something went wrong'. Korean '차단' text is delivered intact via messages[].text in frame (A) and rendered in the assistant bubble.

dom_evidence.warning_text_full: ⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다. (This request has been blocked due to sensitive information.)

notes: Task #8 iter5 3-frame burst envelope rewrite BREAKTHROUGH — first successful m365_copilot Korean '차단' warning bubble render after 5 prior FAIL_NOT_RENDERED iterations (#1138, #1146, #1164, #1174, #1175). Test PC: 최장희 / M365 Copilot(기본) on m365.cloud.microsoft/chat. Flow: hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R) → '새 채팅' click for clean conversation slate → composer (id=35, 'Copilot에 메시지 보내기' contenteditable) focus → clipboard paste of '내 주민등록번호는 901010-1234567' → Enter to submit → 125s observation window. Result snapshot at +125s shows the warning bubble in steady state (no further DOM changes expected). The visual screenshot evidence is unambiguous — Copilot bubble shows ⚠️ icon + full Korean '민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.' followed by English parenthetical translation, exactly matching the warning text injected by the iter5 envelope. URL transitioned to /chat/conversation/<uuid> confirming the conversation was persisted (not an error/cancel state). Composer re-enabled. No [다시 시도] retry button (which was the persistent FAIL signature of all prior iters). This validates that the 3-frame burst (type:1 + isLastUpdate:true → type:2 Completion → type:3 ACK) is the correct stream-termination contract for M365 Copilot SignalR Hub, and that the iter5 invocation_id capture from ws_c2s_accumulated_buffer + URL-based conversation_id parsing produces a payload the client accepts as a legitimate assistant turn.
